data.title: Justera Health Announces Auditor Change
data.description:
TORONTO, ONTARIO / ACCESS Newswire / September 5, 2025 / Justera Health Ltd. ("Justera" or the "Company") announces it has changed its auditors form SHIM & Associates LLP ("Former Auditor") to Horizon Assurance LLP ("Successor Auditor") effective August 7, 2025. The Former Auditor resigned as the Company's auditor effective as of June 2, 2025. The Company's Board of Directors has appointed the Successor Auditor as the new auditor until the next Annual General Meeting of Shareholders of the Company. Due to the change of the Company's external auditor, the filing of the audited annual financial statements of the Company which were due July 30, 2025, has been delayed, and the Company plans to file the audited annual financial statements as soon as possible.

The Company confirms that there have been no modified opinions in the Former Auditor's reports for the two most recently completed financial years or for any period subsequent to the most recently completed period for which an audit report was issued. The Company's board of directors and audit committee each approved the resignation of the Former Auditor and the appointment of the Successor Auditor in place of the Former Auditor; there were no reportable events (as defined in National Instrument 51-102) in connection with each of the Former Auditor's audit of the Company which occurred prior to their resignation as auditors of the Company; and the Notice of Change of Auditor was approved by the Company's board of directors.

In accordance with National Instrument 51-102, the Notice of Change of Auditor, together with the required letters from the Former Auditor and the Successor Auditor, have been reviewed by the audit committee and the board of directors and have been filed on SEDAR.

About Justera Health

Established in 2020, Justera is a Canadian company focused on health and wellness. Through its services, innovative products, and strategic partnerships, Justera empowers individuals to prioritize their well-being. Justera's mission is to enhance Canadians' overall well-being with diverse solutions catering to individual needs.

For additional information on Justera Health and other corporate information, please visit the Company's website at https://www.justerahealth.com/

For more information about the Company, please refer to the Company's profile on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.com.

For further information:

Investor Relations & Communications

Joshua Herman, CEO

Tel: (416) 318-6501

Email: info@justerahealth.com

data.title: Five Best: Books on the Purpose of Life
data.description:
By Arthur C. Brooks

The Brothers Karamazov

By Fyodor Dostoevsky (1880)

1. The dysfunctional Karamazov family is at the heart of Fyodor Dostoevsky's magnum opus. In rural 19-century Russia, Fyodor Karamazov is a neglectful father to Dmitri, Ivan and Alyosha. When Fyodor is murdered, suspicion falls on Dmitri. The novel explores the brothers' responses to the killing and the moral dilemmas that ensue. As each young man confronts evil and suffering, Dostoevsky presents three different life purposes: Dmitri seeks sensuality and passion; Ivan, reason and skepticism; Alyosha, faith, love and forgiveness — even of his father's murderer. Dostoevsky gives a fair hearing to each brother's life strategy but in the end anoints sweet Alyosha's self-transcendence — "We are all responsible for all, for all men before all, and I more than all others" — as the superior approach. "If you attain to perfect self-forgetfulness in the love of your neighbor," Dostoevsky writes, "then you will believe without doubt, and no doubt can possibly enter your soul. This has been tried. This is certain."

Autobiography of a Yogi

By Paramahansa Yogananda (1946)

2. Paramahansa Yogananda (1893-1952) was among the first teachers of Hindu religious philosophy to come to the West from India. He arrived as a 27-year-old Hindu monk to speak at a religious conference in Boston in 1920 and moved on to Los Angeles, where he established the Self-Realization Fellowship, an organization devoted to promulgating meditation and Yogananda's philosophy. Yogananda's autobiography introduced millions of Western readers to meditation, yoga and Hindu spirituality. He described a life devoted to finding oneness with the divine through meditation, introspection and balanced living. The purpose of life, he wrote, is self-realization: to know yourself deeply, to be honestly self-aware. Research confirms that reflecting on one's virtues and faults leads to a feeling of progress in life. Yogananda suggested that the benefits are far more profound: "The deeper the self-realization of a man, the more he influences the whole universe by his subtle spiritual vibrations, and the less he himself is affected by the phenomenal flux."

The Way of a Pilgrim

By Anonymous (1884)

3. In the late 19th century, a young man decides to make a pilgrimage to learn his life's meaning. An old monk advises him to venture forth, praying ceaselessly: "Lord Jesus Christ, have mercy on me." With no destination in mind, the pilgrim walks hundreds of miles across Russia, repeating the prayer. The wanderer's identity isn't known but his adventures are documented in "The Way of a Pilgrim," a bedrock text of orthodox Christian spirituality. "The prayer alone filled my consciousness," says the pilgrim, as an understanding of his life emerges with each step. While wandering he supports himself with part-time work, is robbed, helps a young girl flee an arranged marriage and engages in deep conversations with priests. Though scholars debate whether the pilgrim was real or fictional, historical accuracy is beside the point. More important is this volume's unambiguous answer to the quest: The purpose of life is to seek God.

Meditations

By Marcus Aurelius (b. A.D. 121; d. 180)

4. The reign of Marcus Aurelius, who was the emperor of Rome from A.D. 161-180, was a period of almost constant war. While in the field with his armies, the emperor kept a private journal of his reflections on life. These thoughts were preserved by those close to him and circulated centuries later by Greek scholars. "Meditations" has become a handbook of Stoicism, a philosophy for virtuous living. The purpose of life, according to Marcus Aurelius, is to do what needs to be done. "Meditations" is about leading an honorable life despite inevitable adversities. The book is filled with valuable nuggets: "In the morning when thou risest unwillingly, let this thought be present: I am rising to the work of a human being." And this: "Accept everything which happens, and do thy duty, and speak the truth with a good will." Duty, resilience and self-control, Marcus Aurelius told himself, were the foremost virtues. Many philosophers, from Aristotle centuries before to Hegel centuries after, have argued that these are the keys to a happy life.

The Gay Science

By Friedrich Nietzsche (1882)

5. "God is dead." Friedrich Nietzsche's bleak line is among the most memorable in "The Gay Science," his collection of essays, poetry and cultural criticism. While writing the book, the German philosopher was savoring what he called a rebirth after a bout of illness and creative stagnation. "The Gay Science" marks Nietzsche's emergence from skepticism and suffering to an optimistic affirmation of life. The pursuit of knowledge, he wrote, should be joyful or gay — fröhliche in German. So what could be joyful about God being dead? Nietzsche didn't mean this literally. Rather, he argued, the Enlightenment, with its focus on science and reason, had killed belief in the spiritual realm, along with the idea that life has any kind of transcendent or divine purpose. At this, one might naturally despair. But Nietzsche offers an alternative, sunnier prescription: "To laugh at oneself." When we find the humor in our own futility, "laughter will then have formed an alliance with wisdom." He asks readers to consider that life has no purpose but is simply meant to be lived each day. Stop looking for some deep meaning, he suggests, and smile at the absurdity of it all.

data.title: Airbus Logged Strong August Aircraft Deliveries — Market Talk
data.description: Airbus's commercial aircraft deliveries in August bring encouraging news for the European plane maker during a typically slow month, RBC Capital Markets analysts write in a research note. The group delivered 61 aircraft to customers last month, up 30% year-on-year. While the company's 434 deliveries so far this year are down 3% from the same period last year, analysts say the strong August figures suggest the group will be able to meet its 2025 target of about 820 aircraft deliveries. The fourth quarter has since 2010 accounted for nearly 33% of Airbus's annual deliveries, the analysts note. If the trend persists, they believe the company should be able to hit its delivery target. Airbus shares trade 1.5% lower at 184.08 euros. (william.gray@wsj.com)

data.title: Regenxbio Shares Rise on Positive Long-Term Data for Hunter Syndrome Drug
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

Regenxbio shares rose in early trading Friday after it revealed positive long-term data for the trials of its Hunter syndrome treatment.

Shares rose 4.7% higher to $9.86.

The biotechnology company announced the new positive data from the Phase I, II and III Campsiite trial of clemidsogene lanparvovec, also known as RGX-121, for the treatment of patients with Mucopolysaccharidosis Type II, also known as Hunter syndrome.

Hunger syndrom is a rare inherited genetic disorder that is part of a group of conditions called lysosomal storage disorders. The disorders, which primarily affect boys, interferes with the body's ability to break down certain complex sugars called glycosaminoglycans.

Regenxbio's 12-month pivotal data showed that a one-time RGX-121 treatment can improve outcomes for patients with the disorder.

The Food and Drug Administration is expected to make a decision on the application by Feb. 8 of next year.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com

data.title: ABM INDUSTRIES INC /DE/ SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
ABM Industries Inc., a leading provider of integrated facility solutions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter, showcasing robust financial and operational performance. The company experienced significant growth across various segments, driven by organic expansion, strategic acquisitions, and operational efficiencies.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $2,224.0 million, an increase of $129.8 million or 6.2% compared to the prior year period, driven by organic growth and acquisitions.
  • Operating Profit: $83.4 million, an increase of $46.0 million compared to the prior year period, primarily due to revenue increases across all industry groups and operational efficiencies.
  • Net Income: $41.8 million, an increase of $37.1 million compared to the prior year period, reflecting improved operating performance.
  • Net Income Per Common Share - Basic: $0.67, compared to $0.07 in the prior year period, indicating significant improvement in profitability.
  • Net Income Per Common Share - Diluted: $0.67, compared to $0.07 in the prior year period, reflecting enhanced earnings performance.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments:
    • The Business & Industry (B&I) segment saw a revenue increase of 2.8% to $1,038.7 million, driven by client expansions and strategic pricing decisions.
    • The Manufacturing & Distribution (M&D) segment experienced an 8.4% increase to $408.9 million, attributed to business expansion and new wins.
    • Aviation revenues rose by 8.7% to $291.8 million due to new business and scope expansions.
    • Education revenues increased by 3.0% to $235.1 million, driven by net new business and scope expansions.
    • Technical Solutions revenues grew by 19.0% to $249.5 million, with organic growth and contributions from recent acquisitions.
  • Operational Efficiencies: Operational efficiencies were noted in the Aviation and Education segments, contributing to improved profit margins. The Education segment, in particular, saw a 109 basis point increase in operating profit margin due to better management of overtime, materials, and administrative costs.
  • Future Outlook: The company has launched a restructuring program in the fourth quarter of 2025 aimed at streamlining operations and improving support function efficiency. This initiative is expected to deliver approximately $35.0 million in annualized cost savings once fully implemented in early 2026.
  • Segment Performance:
    • The Aviation segment's operating profit increased by 11.0% due to contract mix and operational efficiencies.
    • The Education segment's operating profit rose by 17.2%, driven by operational efficiencies.
    • Technical Solutions saw an 8.5% increase in operating profit, although the margin slightly decreased due to service mix and higher amortization of intangible assets.
  • Geographical Performance: The B&I segment's revenue growth was supported by client expansions both domestically and internationally, indicating strong performance across different regions.
  • Management Commentary: Management highlighted strategic pricing decisions and proactive contract extensions as key factors in maintaining and expanding customer accounts, particularly within the B&I and M&D segments.
  • Restructuring Program: The restructuring program initiated in the fourth quarter of 2025 is part of the company's strategy to align its cost structure with strategic growth objectives, aiming for enhanced organizational effectiveness.

SEC Filing: ABM INDUSTRIES INC /DE/ [ ABM ] - 10-Q - Sep. 05, 2025
